title: microrhynchus nudicaulis var. divaricatus
intro: Microrhynchus nudicaulis var. divaricatus, commonly known as the hairy rampion, is a variety of plant that belongs to the bellflower family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing pain. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving coughs, and soothing digestive issues. In herbal preparations, hairy rampion is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
